data.title: Analysis: There are frequent notices of tax refunds for trading Hong Kong and US stocks, but the legal aspect of taxing overseas income is not sudden
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: Since March this year, many Chinese residents who have invested in Hong Kong and US stocks have received notices from local tax authorities, prompting them to self-check their personal income at home and abroad and file tax returns in a timely manner. On social platforms, some taxpayers posted that they had completed the personal income tax payment for cross-border investment income at a 20% tax rate before June 30. China's global taxation has always had a legal basis. However, it is understood that compared with previous years, this year's related tax payment and tax payment notices are more intensive and cover a wider range than in previous years, and the notification forms include text messages, phone calls, etc. (Zhitong Finance)

data.title: Russian State-Owned Enterprise Rostec Plans to Launch Ruble Stablecoin and Payment Platform on Tron
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: Russian state-owned enterprise Rostec announced that it will launch a stablecoin RUBx and a payment platform RT-Pay on the Tron blockchain, which are 1:1 anchored to the ruble. As a core enterprise of the Russian military-industrial complex, the group will serve as the only issuer and operator of RUBx, and maintain the anchor value through the "real ruble debt" stipulated by law. This move comes at a time when the Russian Central Bank is simultaneously piloting the digital ruble, and has recently allowed financial institutions to provide crypto-related products to qualified investors. (CoinDesk)

data.title: UpTop and Bitring officially sponsor BNB Hack to support AI and DeFi innovation
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News BNB Chain announced that DeFi protocol UpTop and AI health technology platform Bitring have become the new official sponsors of the BNB Hack hackathon. This joining not only brings two new bounty challenges to developers, but also further expands BNB Hack's technical exploration in fields such as AI, DeSoc, DeSci and DePIN.
• UpTop is a decentralized liquidity protocol built on BNB Chain. Through one-click BNB unilateral staking and IL protection mechanism, it lowers the threshold for DeFi users. UpTop launched a $8,000 bounty challenge this time to encourage developers to expand contracts and innovate governance mechanisms around the next generation of liquidity pools.
• Bitring is a wearable health platform that combines AI bio-monitoring, on-chain incentives and smart contract insurance. Its core product is a 999 gold smart ring that provides 24-hour health data analysis and on-chain protection. This time, Bitring has set up 10 Genesis smart rings (each worth $1,199) as development rewards to encourage the construction of AI modules that can identify diet and nutritional analysis.

data.title: Victims lost $2.37B in 121 crypto hacks in first half of 2025: SlowMist
data.description:
SlowMist’s 2025 mid-year report reveals blockchain security incidents resulted in $2. 373 billion losses across 121 attacks during the first half of 2025. The data shows DeFi protocols remained primary targets while exchange breaches generated the largest individual losses, highlighting persistent vulnerabilities in crypto infrastructure. Blockchain security sector shows mixed patterns The first half of 2025 […]
source: https://www.cryptopolitan.com/2-37b-lost-crypto-hacks-in-first-half-2025/

data.title: Turkey Blocks Access to PancakeSwap Over Unauthorized Crypto Services
data.description:
According to PANews, Turkey's Capital Markets Board (CMB) has blocked access to PancakeSwap, citing unauthorized provision of crypto asset services. This marks the first time Turkey has sanctioned a decentralized exchange (DEX). The ban also extends to the crypto comparison platform CryptoRadar. This decision follows the regulatory authority granted to the CMB last year, allowing it to block unlicensed foreign crypto service platforms targeting Turkish users.

Analysts suggest this move sets a precedent for Turkish regulators to target DEXs, potentially affecting similar platforms like Uniswap and Raydium. Previously, Turkey focused on blocking centralized exchanges that had not applied for local licenses.

data.title: Türkiye imposes sanctions on DEX, PancakeSwap access blocked
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: The Capital Markets Board of Turkey (CMB) has blocked access to PancakeSwap for "unauthorized provision of crypto asset services", marking the first time Turkey has imposed sanctions on DEX. Also banned was the crypto price comparison platform CryptoRadar. The decision was based on the regulatory authorization obtained by CMB last year to block unlicensed overseas crypto service platforms targeting Turkish users. Analysts pointed out that this move set a precedent for Turkish regulators to crack down on DEX, which may affect similar platforms such as Uniswap and Raydium. (Turkiye Today)

data.title: Research firm: Trump's digital asset policy is widely recognized by cryptocurrency investors
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: Research company HarrisX released a market survey result showing that cryptocurrency investors generally approve of Trump's digital asset policy. 81% of respondents said they were familiar with Trump's digital asset policy, and 73% agreed with it. These figures have received the highest support rate among other tested topics. Trump's net support rate on cryptocurrency policy is +56 percentage points, while the net support rate for other key topics is +48 percentage points or even lower. (Cryptoinamerica)

data.title: Data: Base cross-chain bridge funds have seen a net outflow of $4.3 billion this year
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News Artemis Terminal data shows that Coinbase's Layer 2 solution Base cross-chain bridge funds have seen a net outflow of $4.3 billion this year, and has turned from the leader in cross-chain bridge fund inflows in 2024 to the "biggest loser". Ethereum has seen a significant recovery, with a net inflow of $8.5 billion. (CoinDesk)

data.title: Cryptocurrency project financing totaled $10.3 billion in the first half of 2025
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: The total amount of crypto project financing in the first half of 2025 was about 10.3 billion US dollars, surpassing the 9.6 billion US dollars in the whole of 2024. Among them, World Liberty Financial, supported by the Trump family, raised 300 million US dollars in the token sale completed on January 20 (the day Trump was sworn in as president), ranking third among all financing projects. (DLnews)

data.title: Thai zoo to celebrate hippopotamus Moo Deng's first birthday
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: Thailand's Khao Kheow Zoo will celebrate the first birthday of the pygmy hippopotamus Moo Deng on July 10. The four-day "Moo Deng First Birthday" Festival will be held at the Hippo Exhibition Hall in Chonburi Province from July 10 to 13. (StraitsTimes) It is reported that Ethereum co-founder Vitalik Buterin is already Moo Deng's adoptive father. He donated 10 million Thai baht to support her growing up in the next two years, and may provide more donations in the future. It is reported that Vitalik Buterin has reserved 88 ETH for Moo Deng and her friends.

data.title: Vitalik: Ethereum’s success has exceeded anyone’s expectations
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: Ethereum co-founder Vitalik Buterin said: Ethereum's success has exceeded anyone's expectations. (Cointelegraph)

data.title: Belgian court sentences three men to 12 years in prison each for cryptocurrency kidnapping
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News - Three men have been sentenced to 12 years in prison for kidnapping the wife of cryptocurrency investor Stephane Winkel in Belgium and have been ordered to pay at least $1.2 million in compensation to the victim. The case is part of a surge in cryptocurrency-related kidnappings across Europe, and Belgian law enforcement agencies are hunting for the suspected mastermind behind multiple attacks.
Prosecutors at the Brussels Criminal Court said the three kidnapped Stephane Winkel's spouse outside his home, forced him into a French-registered van, and then sped toward the coast. Stephane Winkel immediately called the police, and local police successfully crashed the vehicle out of a ditch near Bruges and arrested the three adults in the car. A teenage accomplice suspected of involvement has been transferred to the juvenile court. (The Block)

data.title: PancakeSwap Reports June CAKE Token Burn and Supply Reduction
data.description: According to Odaily, decentralized exchange platform PancakeSwap has released its June report on CAKE token burns. The report reveals that 3,264,684 CAKE tokens were burned, while 652,564 tokens were minted. As of June this year, the net minting of CAKE tokens stands at -2,612,120, marking the 22nd consecutive month of reduced total token supply.

data.title: Solana's DApp Revenue Leads Blockchain Market for Fifth Consecutive Quarter
data.description: According to PANews, data from SolanaFloor reveals that in the second quarter of 2025, decentralized applications (DApps) on the Solana blockchain generated more revenue than the combined total of DApps on all other blockchains. This marks the fifth consecutive quarter where Solana has surpassed all other blockchains in terms of DApp revenue.

data.title: Opposing the "big and beautiful" bill, Musk calls for the release of Epstein documents
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News According to the US "Daily Beast" website, amid the escalating dispute with US President Trump over the "Big and Beautiful" tax and spending bill, entrepreneur Elon Musk called on the US government to fully disclose all documents related to the sexual assault scandal of the late wealthy businessman Jeffrey Epstein. Previously, Musk had hinted that Trump was related to the "Epstein case." According to reports, a few hours before the US House of Representatives voted on the "Big and Beautiful" bill, on the morning of July 3rd local time, in response to a post on a social platform calling for the release of the "unabridged Epstein documents," Musk said he "agreed." (ZhiTong Finance)

data.title: Bitcoin Millionaire Addresses Surge in First Half of 2025
data.description:
According to BlockBeats, the number of Bitcoin addresses holding over $1 million saw a significant increase in the first half of 2025. The total number of millionaire addresses rose by 26,758, climbing from 155,569 at the beginning of the year to 182,327 by the end of June.

Interestingly, most of this growth occurred in the second quarter. During the first quarter, the number of millionaire addresses actually decreased by 13,942, dropping from 155,569 on January 1 to 141,627 by March 31. The recovery of high-value wallet addresses reflects strong buying activity during the market downturn earlier this year and a resurgence in institutional demand following the launch of a U.S. spot Bitcoin ETF.

data.title: The number of Bitcoin millionaire addresses increased by 26,758 in the first half of 2025
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: In the first half of 2025, the number of addresses holding more than $1 million in Bitcoin increased significantly, with the number of Bitcoin millionaire addresses increasing from 155,569 at the beginning of the year to 182,327 at the end of June, an increase of 26,758 in total. (Finbold)

data.title: Analysis: BTC long-term holders show patience with the market, perhaps waiting for higher prices
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News Despite the recent sell-off in the crypto market, Bitcoin's long-term supply remains strong, which may suggest that prices will continue to rise. Currently, about 45% of the supply has not been circulated for at least three years, the same as in February 2024. Since May 2024, the five-year unused supply has stabilized at 30%. Analysts believe that despite the selling of long-term holders (as they usually do when prices rise), the data shows that this group has not significantly changed its overall trading behavior in more than a year, which means that many are waiting for higher prices before taking further action. (CoinDesk)
